references.text: Kladwong, P, P Chantaranothai & DA Simpson. 2018. Nomenclatural notes on the family Smilacaceae in Thailand. Kew Bulletin 73: 28.
Koyama, T, 1984. A taxonomic revision of the genus Heterosmilax (Smilacaceae). Brittonia 36: 184-205.
Koyama, T. 1960. Materials towards a monograph of the genus Smilax. Quarterly Journal of the Taiwan Museum 13: 1-61.
Qi, Z, P Li & C Fu. 2013. New combinations and a new name in Smilax for species of Heterosmilax in Eastern and Southeast Asian Smilacaceae (Liliales). Phytotaxa, 117: 58-60

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 8 June 2026

species.citation: Wall. ex A.DC. , Monogr. Phan. 1 (1878) 195. = Smilax bracteata C.Presl var. verruculosa (Merr.) T.Koyama , Quart. J. Taiwan Mus. 10 (1957) 18 ; Quart. J. Taiwan Mus. 13 (1960) 12; ≡ Smilax verruculosa Merr. , PJS 5c (1910) 173 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 209. = Smilax williamsii Merr. , PJS 5c (1910) 173 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 209.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, Borneo, China, India, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Benguet, Ifugao, Mountain Province, Pampanga, Quezon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Davao, Lanao del Sur.
species.notes: Note by P.B. Pelser (16-Aug-15): Smilax williamsii is considered a distinct species in the World Checklist of Selected Plant Families.

species.citation: C.Presl , Reliq. Haenk. 1 (1827) 131 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 207; Kladwong et al., Kew Bull. 73 (2018) 28; Type: Haenke s.n. (PRC, holo), Luzon. var. bracteata. Distribution: Cambodia, China, Japan, Laos, Malay Peninsula, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Aurora, Bataan, Benguet, Bulacan, Nueva Ecija, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Davao, MINDORO. Notes: Lowland and medium elevation thickets and secondary forests. Native. var. heterophylla Merr. & Quisumb.; Type: BS 45333 Ramos & Edaño (K, NY), Luzon: Aurora, Casiguran. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Aurora. Native.
species.distribution: Cambodia, China, Japan, Laos, Malay Peninsula, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. CAGAYAN DE SULU, KINAPUSAN, LUZON: Abra (photos), Aurora, Bataan, Benguet, Bulacan, Ifugao, NCR, Nueva Ecija, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Davao, Davao del Sur, MINDORO, PANAY, SABTANG (photos), SAMAR, TURTLE IS.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 1029 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 208. var. china. Distribution: China, Japan, Korea, Philippines, Taiwan. Native. var. kuru Sakag. ex Yamam., J. Soc. Trop. Agric. 11 (1939) 278. Distribution: Hong Kong, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls. Native. var. recticaulis T.Koyama, Dansk Bot. Ark. 23 (1963) 14. Distribution: Philippines, Thailand. LUZON, MINDORO. Notes: In the Cordillera Highlands of Luzon. 1100-2000m. Native.
species.distribution: LUZON: Apayao (photos), Benguet, Ifugao, Ilocos Sur, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Zambales, MINDORO, NEGROS.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 13c (1918) 6 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 208; ≡ Smilax reticulata Elmer , LPB 8 (1915) 2740, nom. illeg., non Desv.; Type: Elmer 13684, Mindanao: Agusan, Cabadbaran (Mt Urdaneta), Sep-1912.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BUCAS GRANDE, BUSUANGA, LUZON: Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Surigao del Norte (photos), Surigao del Sur (photos).

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 13c (1918) 5 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 208; Type: BS 28560 Ramos & Edaño, Luzon: Tayabas, Mt Binuang, May-1917.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Quezon.
species.notes: Forested slopes at low or medium elevation.

species.citation: Blume , Enum. Pl. Javae (1827) 18 ; Merrill & Rolfe, PJS 3c (1908) 96; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 208; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific biodiv. 17 (2024) 13.
species.distribution: Borneo, China, Java, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Philippines. BALABAC, BANCALAN, BUSUANGA, CULION, HOMONHON, LEYTE, LUZON: Apayao (photos), Aurora (photos), Bataan, Benguet, Ifugao, Laguna, Pampanga, Pangasinan, Quirino (photos), Rizal, MINDANAO: Cotabato (photos), Davao, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PANAY.
Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Aurora (photos), Nueva Ecija, Rizal.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 13c (1918) 5 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 209; Coritico et al., PJS 151 (2022) 2440; ≡ Smilax lanceifolia Roxb. var. lucida (Merr.) T.Koyama , Quart. J. Taiwan Mus. 13 (1960) 26.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Apayao, Aurora, Isabela, MINDANAO: Bukidnon.

species.citation: C.Presl , Reliq. Haenk. 1 (1827) 131 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 209; Kladwong et al., Kew Bull. 73 (2018) 28; Type: Haenke s.n. (PRC), Luzon.
species.distribution: Cambodia, India, Java, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. BOHOL, CATANDUANES, JOLO, LUZON: Apayao (photos), Ilocos Norte, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Lanao, Lanao del Sur, Misamis Oriental (photos), PANAY.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 5c (1910) 339 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 209. = Smilax menispermoidea A.DC. var. randaiensis (Hayata) T.Koyama , Quart. J. Taiwan Mus. 13 (1960) 32; ≡ Smilax randaiensis Hayata , J. Coll. Sci. Imp. Univ. Tokyo 30(1) (1911) 362.
A.DC. , Monogr. Phan. 1 (1878) 55. var. riparia. Distribution: China, Philippines. Native.

(A.DC.) P.Li & C.X.Fu , Phytotaxa 117 (2013) 59; ≡ Heterosmilax sumatrensis A.DC. , Monogr. Phan. 1 (1878) 42. = Heterosmilax borneensis A.DC. , Monogr. Phan. 1 (1878) 42 ; Koyama, Brittonia 36 (1984) 203.
species.distribution: Borneo, Cambodia, Java, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Singapore, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. BALABAC, TAWI-TAWI.
Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Laos, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Nicobar Isls, ?Philippines, Thailand, Vietnam.
